# Brian Ward Appraisal

> San Diego certified residential real estate appraiser with 22 years of experience and over 7,000 appraisals completed. Specializing in date of death appraisals, step-up in basis tax valuations, divorce, bankruptcy, estate, and pre-purchase/pre-sale appraisals. Qualified expert witness for litigation, divorce, and bankruptcy matters.

## Company Overview

Brian Ward Appraisal is a certified residential real estate appraisal firm serving San Diego and Riverside Counties. The company specializes in appraisals for purposes outside of mortgage lending, including date of death valuations, step-up in basis tax appraisals, divorce and marriage dissolution, bankruptcy, and appraisals before buying or selling a home.

The company appraises houses, condominiums, and residential vacant land. All appraisals are written to be clearly understood by attorneys, judges, the IRS, accountants, and other non-real estate professionals who rely on them to make critical decisions. This clarity is what sets the company's reports apart and is essential to success when an appraisal is used in legal proceedings, tax filings, or financial negotiations.

Every report is completed by a certified appraiser with 7,000+ appraisals over 22 years. No trainees are used in appraisal preparation.

## Why Choose Brian Ward Appraisal

- Quality Reports: Detailed, descriptive analysis that is logical, clearly written, and able to endure scrutiny from attorneys, judges, the IRS, and opposing counsel.
- Direct Communication: Professional, friendly, and helpful service with direct access to the appraiser working on your report.
- Competitive Rates: Flexible scheduling and fast delivery throughout San Diego and Riverside Counties.
- Certified & Experienced: Every report completed by a certified appraiser with 7,000+ appraisals over 22 years. No trainees.
- Company Pledge: Will not accept an order if the company does not believe it can produce a credible valuation to satisfy the client's need.

## Appraisal Services

- Date of Death Appraisal: Retrospective valuations for IRS step-up in basis, estate tax, and probate
- Divorce Appraisal: Property valuations for equitable division in divorce proceedings
- Bankruptcy Appraisal: Valuations for Chapter 7 and Chapter 13 bankruptcy filings
- Estate Appraisal: Valuations for estate planning, probate, and trust administration
- Tax Appraisal: Valuations for property tax appeals and IRS compliance
- Pre-Purchase Appraisal: Independent valuations before buying a home
- Pre-Sale Appraisal: Valuations to establish listing price before selling
- Family Transaction Appraisal: Fair market value for sales between family members
- Insurance Dispute Appraisal: Valuations for insurance claim disputes
- PMI Removal: Appraisals to remove private mortgage insurance
- Bonds Appraisal: Valuations for bond and surety purposes

## Appraisal Services and Pricing

All pricing information is based on 90%+ of properties falling under this standard pricing structure.

### Appraisal Types and Fees

- $299: Basic Desktop Appraisal
- $449: Desktop Appraisal
- $575: Drive-By Appraisal
- $625: Standard Appraisal (single-family)
- $725: Standard 2-4 Unit Appraisal
- $550: Desktop 2-4 Unit Appraisal

### Appraisal Add-Ons

- $35: Living Area Measure/Certification (per 1,500 SF)
- +30%: Rush Fee (when available)
- $100: Two Client Fee (e.g., both spouses in a divorce)

### How Appraisal Fees Are Determined

Appraisal fees are based on three primary factors:

1. Complexity: Properties with unique characteristics, special features, or complex legal situations require more extensive analysis and research.
2. Time: The amount of field work, research, and report preparation required to complete the appraisal.
3. Liability: The intended use of the appraisal and the potential exposure or liability associated with the valuation conclusion.

### Difference Between Desktop and Standard Appraisals

#### What is the Same

- Degree of Analysis: Both desktop and standard appraisals apply the three approaches to value (sales comparison approach, cost approach, and income approach where applicable).
- Degree of Reporting: Both provide comprehensive written appraisal reports with detailed analysis, market research, and valuation conclusions.
- Credibility: Both are prepared by the same certified appraisers and meet the same professional standards and USPAP requirements.

#### What is Different

- Appraiser Inspections: Desktop appraisals rely on public records, MLS data, and online research without an in-person property inspection. Standard appraisals include a thorough physical inspection of the subject property and comparable properties.
- Client Influence: Desktop appraisals cannot be influenced by the client's proximity or expectations about the appraisal. Standard appraisals may reflect on-site conditions and observations that could influence valuation.

### Desktop Appraisal Appropriate For

- Estate valuations
- Divorce proceedings
- Bail-Bonds
- Bankruptcy filings
- Immigration purposes
- Litigation support
- Second opinions on valuations

### Standard Appraisals Recommended For

- Family property transfers
- Unusual or specialized properties
- Divorce property divisions
- Bankruptcy asset valuations
- Litigation support requiring detailed inspection
- Immigration property valuations
- Bail-bond hearings requiring thorough documentation

### What is Included with Each Report

#### Desktop Appraisal Report Includes

- Comprehensive market analysis of comparable sales
- Property description based on public records and MLS data
- Analysis of current market conditions in the subject's neighborhood
- Valuation using the Sales Comparison Approach
- Cost Approach analysis (where applicable)
- Income Approach analysis (for investment properties)
- Final value opinion with clear reasoning
- Certification and statement of limiting conditions

#### Standard Appraisal Report Includes

- Detailed physical inspection report and photographs
- Comprehensive property description including structural elements, systems, and condition
- Comparable sales analysis with detailed property adjustments
- Neighborhood market analysis with demographic data
- Sales Comparison Approach with detailed adjustments
- Cost Approach with replacement cost analysis
- Income Approach analysis (for investment properties)
- Reconciliation of approaches and final valuation conclusion
- Photographs of subject property and comparable properties
- Professional certification and statement of limiting conditions

## Date of Death Appraisal Service

Date of Death Appraisals, also known as retrospective appraisals, are a significant portion of the company's business. The effective date of a date of death appraisal is some date in the past, usually when a person passed away. Historical appraisals range from one month to over 30 years prior. The company prepares IRS-compliant appraisals at competitive rates.

### Expertise in Historical Appraisals

The company has extensive experience interpreting historical market data using stringent appraisal standards not typically employed in mortgage appraisals. Appraisers conduct thorough research of:

- Historical market conditions and trends at the effective appraisal date
- Comparable properties that sold during the appropriate time period
- Market fluctuations and economic conditions affecting property values
- IRS requirements for estate valuations and stepped-up basis calculations

### Appraisal Types and IRS Requirements

Standard appraisal fees apply to date of death appraisals. Desktop appraisals do not meet IRS requirements except for vacant land. For residential and improved properties, a full Consumer Appraisal or Standard Appraisal is necessary to satisfy IRS guidelines and obtain credibility with tax authorities.

### Beyond Estate Appraisals

The company's services extend beyond estate settlement to include appraisals needed for bankruptcy, litigation, and divorce proceedings. The same historical appraisal expertise applied to date of death valuations serves many other legal and financial purposes.

## Divorce / Marriage Dissolution Appraisal Service

The company specializes in divorce and marriage dissolution property appraisals. The service distinguishes itself through several key differentiators:

- Thorough Research Presented Credibly: Comprehensive research presented in a manner that professional and legal reviewers understand and accept.
- Sensitivity to Dissolution Processes: Understanding of the emotional and financial complexities involved in marriage dissolution with appropriate sensitivity to each assignment.
- Strict Confidentiality: Personal and financial information is protected throughout the appraisal process in accordance with the highest privacy standards.
- Ongoing Support: Consultation and support after report delivery, including potential court testimony if needed.

### Divorce Appraisals vs. Mortgage Appraisals

Divorce appraisals differ significantly from mortgage appraisals. Mortgage lenders often put restrictions and requirements on the appraiser with the goal of ensuring the sale of the loan they are funding in the secondary mortgage market. In contrast, divorce appraisals are tailored specifically to legal needs and are not bound by lender guidelines or restrictions.

### Expertise in Backdated and Retrospective Appraisals

The company has completed over 1,000 backdated and retrospective appraisals, dating back up to 28 years in the past. In many divorce cases, property needs to be appraised as of a specific date in the past, such as the date of separation or the date the marriage began. The company has the expertise and historical market knowledge to handle these complex assignments accurately using stringent appraisal standards.

## Frequently Asked Questions

### What is a real estate appraisal?

An appraisal is an independent, professional estimate of what a home is worth. A certified appraiser reviews the property, compares it to similar homes that have recently sold nearby, and considers current market conditions to arrive at a fair market value. The result is a written report you can use for legal, tax, or personal purposes.

### What types of appraisals do you offer?

Desktop appraisals are done without visiting the property—the appraiser works from public records, photos, and market data. Drive-By appraisals include photographing the exterior without entering the home. Standard appraisals include a full interior and exterior inspection. The company also handles multi-unit properties (2–4 units).

### How much does an appraisal cost?

Fees start at $299 for a Basic Desktop Appraisal and go up to $725 for a Standard 2–4 Unit Appraisal. A Standard single-family appraisal is $625. Fees can vary for complex properties, large acreage, or rush delivery.

### How long does it take to get my report?

Most reports are delivered within 3–5 business days after the inspection, or after you place the order for desktop appraisals. Rush service is available for tight deadlines.

### What is a date-of-death appraisal and when do I need one?

A date-of-death appraisal determines what a home was worth on the date someone passed away. It's used to establish the property's tax basis for the estate, which affects how much capital gains tax the heirs may owe if and when they sell. The IRS typically requires this type of appraisal during estate settlement. The company can do these going back 30 or more years, depending on available market data.

### I'm going through a divorce. How does the appraisal process work?

In a divorce, you need an independent value for the home so the property can be divided fairly. The company provides a neutral appraisal that both parties can rely on, and offers a two-client option so both spouses receive the report at a lower combined cost than ordering separately. If your case goes to court, expert witness testimony is also provided. The company has completed over 1,000 divorce-related appraisals.

### What is an estate or trust appraisal?

When someone passes away or a trust is being administered, the real estate usually needs to be valued for probate, tax reporting, or distribution to beneficiaries. An estate appraisal provides that official value. It can be done as of today's date or retroactively as of the date of death. Reports meet court and IRS requirements.

### Can I get an appraisal before I buy or sell a home?

A pre-purchase appraisal gives you an independent value before you make an offer, so you know whether the asking price is fair. A pre-sale appraisal helps you set a realistic listing price—it's more thorough than a real estate agent's market analysis and can prevent costly pricing mistakes. Either way, it's a relatively small investment that can save you thousands.

### Do I need an appraisal for a bankruptcy filing?

Yes. If you own real estate and are filing Chapter 7, 11, or 13 bankruptcy, the court requires a current market value for your property. The company's appraisals are court-compliant and meet the documentation standards that bankruptcy trustees and judges expect.

### Can you help with property tax appeals?

Yes. If you believe your county-assessed value is too high, an independent appraisal can support your appeal. The company also prepares appraisals for gift tax purposes and stepped-up basis calculations for inherited property. All tax-related appraisals are documented to IRS standards.

### What is a family transaction appraisal?

When you're buying or selling property to a family member, the IRS expects the transaction to be at fair market value. An independent appraisal protects everyone involved by documenting that the price is legitimate, which helps avoid tax penalties or gift tax issues down the road.

### Can an appraisal help me get rid of PMI?

Yes. Private mortgage insurance (PMI) is required when you have less than 20% equity in your home. If your home's value has gone up since you bought it, a new appraisal can prove you've reached that 20% threshold—and your lender may remove the PMI, potentially saving you hundreds of dollars per month.

### Are your appraisals accepted by courts and the IRS?

Yes. All appraisals comply with USPAP (Uniform Standards of Professional Appraisal Practice), which is the standard recognized by courts, the IRS, and financial institutions. The company has over 22 years of experience preparing reports for legal and tax proceedings.

### Do I need to be home during the inspection?

You don't have to be, but you're welcome to be present. The appraiser will photograph the home and note its condition, features, and any upgrades. If you're there, you can point out improvements that might not be obvious—like a new roof, updated plumbing, or a remodeled kitchen.

### What do you need from me to get started?

Just the property address, what the appraisal is for (divorce, estate, buying/selling, etc.), and a specific date if you need a retrospective value. You can call (888) 844-1733, email contact@brianward.com, or fill out the online contact form.

### What areas do you cover?

The company serves all of San Diego County and parts of Riverside County—over 56 communities in total.

## Service Area

Brian Ward Appraisal provides comprehensive real estate appraisal services throughout San Diego and Riverside Counties. With 22 years of experience and more than 7,000 completed appraisals, the company understands the local real estate market and provides detailed, credible valuations that hold up to professional scrutiny.

### San Diego County Communities Served

San Diego, Chula Vista, Oceanside, Escondido, El Cajon, Carlsbad, Vista, San Marcos, National City, Encinitas, La Mesa, Santee, Poway, La Jolla, Imperial Beach, Coronado, Lemon Grove, Solana Beach, Del Mar, Rancho Santa Fe, Ramona, Alpine, Descanso, Spring Valley, Valley Center

### Riverside County Communities Served

Riverside, Moreno Valley, Corona, Temecula, Murrieta, Menifee, Lake Elsinore, Hemet, San Jacinto, Perris, Norco, Jurupa Valley, Eastvale, Beaumont, Banning, Calimesa, Canyon Lake, Wildomar, Winchester, French Valley, Sun City, Anza, Aguanga, De Luz, Rainbow

## Expert Witness and Litigation Support

Brian Ward serves as a qualified expert witness in litigation, divorce, and bankruptcy matters. With 22 years of appraisal experience and over 7,000 completed assignments, Brian has provided expert testimony and litigation support for both lenders and borrowers. Whether the matter involves a disputed property value, a contested appraisal, or a complex valuation question, the company provides credible, well-documented opinions that hold up under cross-examination.

Brian Ward also operates date-of-death.com, a dedicated resource focused exclusively on date of death appraisals. The site provides detailed information about IRS step-up in basis requirements, the retrospective appraisal process, and how date of death valuations work for estate settlement and tax purposes. It is a separate brand but operated by the same certified appraiser.

- Website: https://www.date-of-death.com
